data.title: As a pre-tool-use hook user, all 7 pre-* dispatchers fail open on unexpected crashes via shared ERR handler
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Adds fail-open ERR trap to all 7 pre-* dispatchers by sourcing `hook-error-handler.sh` and registering `_dso_hook_err_handler`. Currently these dispatchers have NO ERR trap at all, meaning any unexpected crash exits non-zero and blocks the tool call.

**Why**: Pre-* dispatchers gate every Claude tool invocation. An unexpected crash without a trap blocks the user's workflow with no diagnostic information. Adding the shared handler makes them fail open and logs the crash for debugging.

**Scope**:
- IN: `dispatchers/pre-bash.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-all.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-edit.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-write.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-enterplanmode.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-exitplanmode.sh`, `dispatchers/pre-taskoutput.sh` — each gets a `source "$HOOKS_LIB_DIR/hook-error-handler.sh"` call with fail-open guard, followed by ERR trap registration
- OUT: Hook lib functions called by dispatchers (S4), enforcement boundary (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 7 pre-* dispatcher files source `hook-error-handler.sh` using a fail-open guard (source failure does not block the tool call) and register `_dso_hook_err_handler` as their ERR trap
  ← Satisfies: "All 7 pre-* dispatchers have fail-open ERR traps using the shared handler"
- When this story is complete, an unexpected crash anywhere in a pre-* dispatcher (not in a lib function) causes a JSONL log entry to be written to `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` and the dispatcher exits 0, not blocking the tool call
  ← Satisfies: "All 7 pre-* dispatchers have fail-open ERR traps using the shared handler"
- When this story is complete, all existing tests pass and unit tests are written and passing for ERR handling in pre-* dispatchers

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Source guard pattern required: `source "$HOOKS_LIB_DIR/hook-error-handler.sh" 2>/dev/null || true` — if the handler file is missing (partial install), the dispatcher must still run without blocking. Do not use bare `source` without a guard.
- [Reliability] ERR traps in pre-* dispatchers must use `exit 0` semantics (not `return 0`) since dispatchers are top-level scripts, not functions
- [Testing] Test the ERR path by inserting a deliberate `false` command after handler registration and verifying the dispatcher exits 0 and a JSONL entry is written

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
